summary: Build guru/prompt.py (chunk formatting + system prompt template)
description:
## Goal

Build `guru/prompt.py` — formats retrieved chunks and assembles the system prompt that enforces the citation contract.

## File Location

`guru/prompt.py`

## Interface

```python
def build_prompt(query: str, chunks: list[RetrievedChunk], prefs: UserPreferences) -> str
def format_chunks(chunks: list[RetrievedChunk]) -> str
```

## Chunk Format

```
---
[Tradition | Text | Section]
Translator: {translator}
Concepts: {concepts}
Confidence: {tier}

{body}
---
```

## System Prompt

Persona: "You are Guru, a scholar of comparative esoteric and religious traditions."

Includes:
- `ACTIVE TRADITIONS:` summary derived from `prefs`
- Citation rules: every claim cites `[Tradition | Text | Section]`; quotes use quotation marks; cross-tradition claims cite all relevant traditions; ◇ Proposed edges require hedging language; never fabricate; never cite outside active scope.
- `RETRIEVED CONTEXT:` block
- `USER QUERY:` block

Use the template in docs/guru-implementation.md §"Stage 5 Prompt Assembly" verbatim as the starting point.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Output prompt is deterministic for fixed input
- Every chunk in context block carries full citation metadata
- Active-traditions line correctly reflects whitelist/blacklist
- System prompt enforces all four key rules from docs/guru-implementation.md
